obligations.reason: The visible scope exposes debug_req_i, debug_mode_o, set_debug_pc_o, dpc_q, DCSR/DPC CSRs, and top-level debug_req gating. Check whether an external debug subject can force privileged debug behavior without an authorization guard.
obligations.subject: external debug requester via debug_req_i
obligations.object: debug mode state and PC control path
obligations.expected_guard: Debug entry and debug PC redirection should be mediated by a debug authorization/lifecycle guard or equivalent platform policy, not only by request assertion and pipeline state.
obligations.candidate_path: debug_req_i -> debug_req -> csr_regfile.debug_req_i -> debug_mode_q/debug_mode_o/set_debug_pc_o -> controller/front-end PC control
state_conditions_to_check: debug_req_i assertion while not in debug mode
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer is truncated, so exact write/read case logic and debug FSM conditions must be inspected in source.

obligations.reason: csr_regfile contains many machine-level protected registers and software CSR access inputs. Verify decode logic applies privilege checks based on CSR address privilege bits/current privilege before read/write enables update protected state.
obligations.subject: software CSR instruction path
obligations.object: machine CSRs: mstatus, mtvec, medeleg, mideleg, mie, mip, mscratch, mepc, mcause, mtval, mcycle/minstret and machine trap/interrupt policy
obligations.expected_guard: Writes and reads of machine-mode CSRs should be blocked or trap when current privilege is below M-mode; writes should additionally honor CSR read-only encoding.
obligations.candidate_path: CSR instruction path csr_addr_i/csr_wdata_i/fu_op/commit_ack_i -> csr_we/csr_wdata -> machine CSR registers and outputs
obligations.uncertainty: Current privilege signal name is not fully visible in fact layer; inspect csr_regfile privilege tracking and read_access_exception/csr_we generation.

obligations.reason: Signals satp_ppn_o, asid_o, en_translation_o, sum_o, mxr_o, tvm_o, tw_o, tsr_o indicate supervisor-visible memory-permission policy. Check U-mode cannot configure/read protected S-mode state and that machine restriction bits are enforced.
obligations.subject: software CSR instruction path from U/S execution
obligations.object: supervisor trap and translation state including SATP, SSTATUS fields, STVEC, SEPC, SIE/SIP
obligations.expected_guard: Supervisor CSRs should be inaccessible from U-mode; S-mode writes to sensitive supervisor controls should be subject to privilege and relevant machine control bits such as TVM/TSR/TW where applicable.
obligations.candidate_path: CSR instruction path -> supervisor CSR cases -> stvec/sscratch/sepc/scause/stval/satp/sstatus/sie/sip updates -> translation/trap outputs
obligations.uncertainty: Need source inspection to determine how umode_i and privilege state are used.

obligations.reason: The package defines debug CSR addresses and csr_regfile has dpc_q, dscratch0_q, dscratch1_q, debug_mode_q, single_step_o. Verify normal CSR access cannot expose or alter debug state unless appropriate debug-mode guard is present.
obligations.subject: software CSR instruction path and/or debug requester
obligations.object: debug CSRs DCSR, DPC, DSCRATCH0, DSCRATCH1 and debug control bits including single_step_o
obligations.expected_guard: Debug CSRs should be readable/writable only when debug_mode_q is active or by an authorized debug module path; normal M/S/U CSR instructions should not access them outside debug mode.
obligations.candidate_path: CSR address CSR_DCSR/CSR_DPC/CSR_DSCRATCH0/CSR_DSCRATCH1 -> dcsr/dpc_q/dscratch*_q read/write cases -> csr_rdata_o/csr_wdata updates
obligations.uncertainty: DCSR register signal may be typed/packed rather than listed as *_q; inspect cases around debug CSR addresses.

obligations.reason: The read mux is a common exposure point. The visible scope includes read_access_exception and many protected CSR registers; check if csr_rdata_o is suppressed/exceptioned for unauthorized addresses rather than leaking values.
obligations.subject: software CSR read path
obligations.object: CSR read mux outputs csr_rdata_o for protected machine/supervisor/debug/performance state
obligations.expected_guard: Read access should enforce CSR privilege and counter-enable policy; sensitive trap scratch/PC or translation configuration should not be exposed to lower privilege.
obligations.candidate_path: CSR read path csr_addr_i -> csr_rdata_o including m/s scratch, epc, cause, tval, satp, counter/perf values
obligations.uncertainty: Need full read case logic; fact layer only lists case line anchors.

obligations.reason: CSR outputs directly influence load/store and instruction translation. Verify lower privilege cannot alter translation policy and that policy changes do not leave stale or inconsistent permission state.
obligations.subject: software CSR write path
obligations.object: virtual memory permission controls: SATP mode/PPN/ASID, SUM, MXR, MPRV, translation enable outputs
obligations.expected_guard: Translation and memory access policy updates must require sufficient privilege, and changes to SATP or relevant status bits should be synchronized with required flush/TLB invalidation behavior or explicit architectural mediation.
state_conditions_to_check: SATP write in S-mode with TVM set
required_evidence_slots: tvm guard for S-mode SATP/SFENCE if applicable
obligations.uncertainty: The fact layer shows outputs but not complete update logic; inspect write cases and ariane connections.

obligations.reason: Signals mret, sret, dret, debug_mode_q, eret_o, epc_o, tsr_o indicate mode transition controls. Check illegal lower-privilege returns cannot change privilege or PC without required guards.
obligations.subject: software instruction stream / debug return path
obligations.object: privilege mode, debug mode, exception PC and return control
obligations.expected_guard: Exception return operations must only be permitted from appropriate privilege/debug state: MRET in M-mode, SRET in S-mode when not forbidden by TSR, DRET only in debug mode.
obligations.candidate_path: csr_regfile exception/return path mret/sret/dret -> privilege/status/debug state updates -> eret_o/epc_o/trap_vector_base_o
state_conditions_to_check: S-mode executing SRET with TSR set
obligations.uncertainty: Instruction decode may be partly outside scope; inspect csr_regfile input fu_op and exception interactions.

obligations.reason: The package defines CSR_PMPCFG0 and CSR_PMPADDR0. Even if implementation is minimal, verify whether accesses are trapped/ignored or correctly guarded and whether any PMP state affects memory permission checks.
obligations.subject: software CSR write path
obligations.object: PMP configuration/address registers and memory access-control policy
obligations.expected_guard: PMP configuration/address CSRs should be writable only by M-mode and locked PMP entries should prevent subsequent writes until reset; LSU/fetch use sites should consume PMP permissions consistently.
obligations.candidate_path: CSR write path -> PMPCFG0/PMPADDR0 decode if implemented -> memory access permission outputs/LSU checks
state_conditions_to_check: PMP lock bit set then rewrite attempt
obligations.uncertainty: No PMP state signals appear in provided signal list; obligation includes checking whether defined addresses are unsupported safely or implemented elsewhere.

obligations.reason: csr_regfile exposes perf_addr_o, perf_data_o, perf_we_o and receives perf_data_i; package defines user counter addresses and machine counters. Check counter access is not globally exposed without counter-enable mediation.
obligations.subject: software CSR and perf counter path
obligations.object: cycle/instret/performance counters and perf counter external interface
obligations.expected_guard: Performance counter reads/writes should respect privilege/counter-enable policy (mcounteren/scounteren if implemented) and machine-only counter CSRs should not be writable/readable by lower modes unless delegated/enabled.

obligations.uncertainty: Counter-enable registers are defined but corresponding q signals are not listed; inspect if omitted or unsupported.

obligations.reason: Custom CSRs at 0x700/0x701 drive cache enable outputs. Check they are not accessible from U/S mode unless explicitly intended and that reset defaults/flush interactions are safe.
obligations.subject: software CSR write path
obligations.object: custom cache-control CSRs CSR_ICACHE/CSR_DCACHE and icache_en_o/dcache_en_o
obligations.expected_guard: Cache enable/disable/configuration CSRs should require machine privilege or platform-authorized mode and should not be writable by lower privilege to alter system behavior or bypass expected cache/TLB maintenance.
obligations.candidate_path: CSR write path -> icache_q/dcache_q -> icache_en_o/dcache_en_o and flush controls
state_conditions_to_check: cache disable/enable while pending memory operations
obligations.uncertainty: Custom CSR privilege expectations may be platform-specific; use CSR address range and downstream impact to judge expected guard.

obligations.object: committed/fetched PC trace and cycle/timing state
obligations.expected_guard: Trace outputs or tracer programs should be disabled, synthesized out, or gated by debug/trace authorization before exposing PCs or execution timing outside the core.

obligations.reason: Multiple protected CSR registers and debug state are reset-controlled. Check asynchronous/synchronous reset branches and first-cycle mtvec load behavior do not create an unsafe debug/privilege or exposure state.
obligations.subject: reset/lifecycle logic
obligations.expected_guard: Reset defaults should place protected state in a safe privilege/permission configuration: debug inactive, translation/cache controls intentional, trap vectors initialized, no stale debug/scratch PC exposure.
